shared_properties: Each HUD state occupies a 24x8 px strip made from three 8x8 torch icons aligned to the upper-left HUD anchor.
Core palette metadata remains primary #3B3347, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39 while icon colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Icon silhouettes preserve the healing torch language so health information matches world pickups.
differences: Atlas includes hp3, hp2, hp1, and hp0 strips with filled torches using #F5A623 and empty torches using #3D2B00.
Filled-to-empty transitions change only flame fill and ember pixels while the holder silhouette stays stable.
The strip is intentionally compact so it fits the 8 px margin from the upper-left corner.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the three-slot HP display, 8 px HUD sizing, and immediate update behavior for damage and healing.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the warm-against-dark dungeon contrast so health remains visible without a giant panel.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the torch silhouette source used by the healing pickup so HUD and world feedback feel unified.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: ImageBundle for static HUD art and SpriteSheetBundle for multi-state overlays and gauges that swap atlas indices.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load UI textures during the shared Loading state and store handles in a HudVisualRegistry keyed by asset_key so gameplay systems never parse filenames.
runtime_notes: Anchor UI nodes to whole pixels in the 1280x720 output space while the gameplay layer is upscaled from 320x180.
Keep the friction gauge, HP strip, and timer on a dedicated HUD layer above parallax and hazard sprites.
Swap overlay states by atlas index or visibility flags instead of rebuilding UI trees during pause, fail, or clear transitions.

context.usage: Upper-left health readout that swaps among four authored states.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 96x8 px with 4 frames of 24x8 px and 0 px spacing.
Each state contains exactly three 8x8 torch icons and the count of filled flames matches the hp3-hp0 label.
Filled flames use #F5A623 and empty flames use #3D2B00 with no extra colors beyond the listed set.
